companions.name: Aromabug
companions.description: Contrary to what their appearance might suggest, Aromabugs give off a delicious aroma when they die.

companions.name: Buttontail
companions.description: The flowery tail of this hare seems to blossom when they reach adulthood. Its function remains somewhat unclear.

companions.name: Grassflopper
companions.description: Masters of camouflage, these frogs are largely ignored by carnivorous predators, but have made themselves a target for unsuspecting ruminants.

companions.name: Red Leggybug
companions.description: Leggybugs' legs keep growing throughout their lives.

companions.name: Saladmander
companions.description: Saladmanders are born in the hearts of lettuce, and unfortunately, the farmer's vegetable garden is often their first meal.

companions.name: Tortrock
companions.description: Tortrocks usually have three stones on their shells. It is said that finding one with four stones brings good luck.

companions.name: Woolybell
companions.description: The Woolybell's bell grows as they do. It is said that a herd of adult Woolybells makes more noise than a mountain storm.
